channel.title: post on Peter Gao
channel.description: Recent content in post on Peter Gao

item.title: Gauss-Hermite Quadrature and Laplace Approximations
item.description: There always seems to be a better vantage point–even well-established photo spots seem to be missing something (perhaps just because they are so widely accepted!). Still, that’s the challenge at hand–to pick your spot. Anyways, today I feel like the best viewpoint for understanding the uses of Gauss-Hermite quadrature in statistics comes from a 1994 paper, “A Note on Gauss-Hermite Quadrature,” by Qing Liu and Donald A. Pierce. Assume we wish to approximate the following integral:

item.title: Graduate Climate Conference 2020
item.description: Excited to share that I’ve been helping with planning for this fall’s (online) Graduate Climate Conference and applications are now open! Please share and feel free to send me any suggestions about making online conferences accessible/meaningful!
